name: Loop around the Vegas de Coria area
name_original: Vuelta por la zona de Vegas de Coria
description: This 9.5-mile moderate mountain biking route near Vegas de Coria offers varied terrain, 1209 feet of elevation gain, and natural beauty.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Nuñomoral offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, natural pools, and varying elevations. The region features a mix of moderate and difficult mountain bike trails, with some routes exploring areas like Vegas de Coria and others connecting natural pools and reservoirs. Riders can expect a blend of natural scenery and challenging terrain across the area. Best mountain bike trails around Nuñomoral The most popular mountain bike trail is Loop around the Vegas de Coria area, a 9.5 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied terrain of the Vegas de Coria region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Riomalo Natural Pool – Water Reservoir loop from Las Mestas, a moderate 27.3 miles (43.9 km) path. This route features scenic natural pools and a water reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo Natural Pool – Pinofranqueado Town Center loop from Caminomorisco, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail leading through natural pools and connecting to a town center, often completed in about 7 hours. Mountain biking around Nuñomoral is defined by natural pools, river valleys, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Nuñomoral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 13 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Nuñomoral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Nuñomoral?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails in the Nuñomoral area, offering a diverse range of experiences for riders.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Nuñomoral?
A: The trails around Nuñomoral are predominantly moderate to difficult. You'll find 10 moderate routes and 10 difficult routes, catering to riders looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Nuñomoral?
A: While most trails are moderate to difficult, there is one easy mountain bike route available in Nuñomoral, suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the typical lengths of mountain bike trails in Nuñomoral?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013478766/loop-around-the-vegas-de-coria-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around the Vegas de Coria area&lt;/a&gt; is about 15 km, while more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de la Genera – Views of the Sierra de Francia loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt; can extend to over 82 km.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Nuñomoral?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Nuñomoral are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riomalo Natural Pool – Water Reservoir loop from Las Mestas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Riscosillo – Pimpollar Track - Section 1 loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The Nuñomoral area is known for its natural pools and river valleys. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4511250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera&lt;/a&gt; waterfall or various natural swimming spots mentioned in route descriptions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several interesting landmarks. You could visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/433119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of La Alberca&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of the Desert of San José de Las Batuecas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Nuñomoral?
A: The mountain bike trails in Nuñomoral are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 13 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic natural pools, and the challenging climbs and descents.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Nuñomoral?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning rides are recommended, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike routes in Nuñomoral?
A: Yes, the hilly terrain provides several opportunities for panoramic views. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4386882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Blanco Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1590368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking on trails in Nuñomoral?
A: Generally, permits are not required for mountain biking on the established trails around Nuñomoral. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific areas or protected natural parks you plan to enter.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route, 'Vuelta por la zona de Vegas de Coria', is estimated to take around 1 hour and 17 minutes to complete. This duration is an average and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is characteristic of the Cáceres province, featuring a variety of natural trails. You can expect sections through Mediterranean forests, cork oaks, and holm oaks. Given the region's rugged orography, some parts may have steep slopes and potentially loose, dusty, or rocky surfaces, offering an authentic wilderness experience.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this route is best suited for mountain bikers with some experience. While not overly technical, the varied terrain and elevation changes mean it requires a reasonable level of fitness and basic riding skills. Beginners might find some sections challenging.

faqs.question: What can I see along the 'Vuelta por la zona de Vegas de Coria' route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter natural beauty typical of the Las Hurdes region. Key highlights include the Fuente de Arrolobos, a local facility, and the Natural Pool of Vegas de Coria, a lovely spot to enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Vegas de Coria area is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable riding and enjoying the natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the 'Vuelta por la zona de Vegas de Coria' route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on natural trails in this region. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, especially when passing through areas with livestock or other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to ride this route?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a specific permit or have to pay any entrance fees to ride this mountain biking route in the Vegas de Coria area. It's a public trail accessible to all. However, always respect private property and local regulations if you venture off the main path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the 'Vuelta por la zona de Vegas de Coria' route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact route isn't detailed, Vegas de Coria is a village with various public parking options. You can usually find parking within the village itself, and then cycle to the nearest access point for the route. Look for designated parking areas or consult local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other local paths. You will find yourself riding along parts of the Cañada del Río, Calle Olivar, and Camino Nuevo, which are official trails in the area. This offers a varied experience and connects you to the broader network of paths around Vegas de Coria.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Vegas de Coria is a village in the Las Hurdes region, and as such, it offers basic facilities. You can find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the village. These establishments provide convenient places to refuel or stay overnight before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers captivating natural beauty with stunning views of the rugged orography characteristic of the Las Hurdes region. You'll experience landscapes filled with Mediterranean forests, cork oaks, and holm oaks, and potentially catch glimpses of the river meander near Vegas de Coria, providing a true wilderness adventure.
